# Cdm { #cdmconnector.Cdm }

```python
Cdm(
    tables,
    *,
    cdm_name,
    cdm_version=None,
    cdm_schema=None,
    write_schema=None,
    achilles_schema=None,
    source,
)
```

OMOP CDM reference: holds a mapping of table names to Ibis table expressions
plus metadata (name, version, schemas, source).

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [achilles_schema](#cdmconnector.Cdm.achilles_schema) | Schema for Achilles tables (optional). |
| [cdm_schema](#cdmconnector.Cdm.cdm_schema) | Schema where CDM tables live. |
| [con](#cdmconnector.Cdm.con) | Underlying Ibis connection, or None if not database-backed. |
| [name](#cdmconnector.Cdm.name) | CDM name (e.g. from cdm_source or user). |
| [source](#cdmconnector.Cdm.source) | Source (connection) for this CDM. |
| [tables](#cdmconnector.Cdm.tables) | List of table names in this CDM. |
| [version](#cdmconnector.Cdm.version) | OMOP CDM version (5.3 or 5.4). |
| [write_schema](#cdmconnector.Cdm.write_schema) | Schema for write/cohort tables. |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [disconnect](#cdmconnector.Cdm.disconnect) | Disconnect the underlying source. |
| [flatten](#cdmconnector.Cdm.flatten) | Flatten the CDM into a single observation table. |
| [sample](#cdmconnector.Cdm.sample) | Subset the CDM to a random sample of n persons. |
| [select_tables](#cdmconnector.Cdm.select_tables) | Return a new Cdm with only the given tables (subset). |
| [snapshot](#cdmconnector.Cdm.snapshot) | Snapshot CDM metadata. Executes and returns a 1-row pandas DataFrame. |
| [subset](#cdmconnector.Cdm.subset) | Subset the CDM to a set of person IDs. |
| [subset_cohort](#cdmconnector.Cdm.subset_cohort) | Subset the CDM to individuals in one or more cohorts. |

### disconnect { #cdmconnector.Cdm.disconnect }

```python
Cdm.disconnect(drop_write_schema=False)
```

Disconnect the underlying source.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type   | Description                                                             | Default   |
|-------------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| drop_write_schema | bool   | If True, drop write-schema tables before disconnecting (default False). | `False`   |

### flatten { #cdmconnector.Cdm.flatten }

```python
Cdm.flatten(domain=None, include_concept_name=True)
```

Flatten the CDM into a single observation table.

Transforms selected domain tables into a common schema (person_id,
observation_concept_id, start_date, end_date, type_concept_id, domain)
and unions them. Recommended only for filtered or small CDMs.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                 | Type                | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Default   |
|----------------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| domain               | list\[str\] or None | Domains to include. Must be a subset of: "condition_occurrence", "drug_exposure", "procedure_occurrence", "measurement", "visit_occurrence", "death", "observation". Default is condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence. | `None`    |
| include_concept_name | bool                | If True (default), add observation_concept_name and type_concept_name via the concept table.                                                                                                                                               | `True`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                  | Description                                          |
|--------|-----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
|        | ibis.expr.types.Table | Lazy table expression; use collect() to materialize. |

### sample { #cdmconnector.Cdm.sample }

```python
Cdm.sample(n, seed=None, name='person_sample')
```

Subset the CDM to a random sample of n persons.

Persons are drawn from the person table. The sample table is
inserted into the write schema under the given name and all
clinical tables are filtered to those persons.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                                 | Default           |
|--------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| n      | int         | Number of persons to include.                                               | _required_        |
| seed   | int or None | Random seed for reproducibility; None uses a random seed.                   | `None`            |
| name   | str         | Name of the table storing the sampled person_ids (default "person_sample"). | `'person_sample'` |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                                     |
|--------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | Cdm    | New CDM with tables subset to the sampled persons (and the sample table added). |

### select_tables { #cdmconnector.Cdm.select_tables }

```python
Cdm.select_tables(*names)
```

Return a new Cdm with only the given tables (subset).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description          | Default   |
|--------|--------|----------------------|-----------|
| *names | str    | Table names to keep. | `()`      |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                    |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------|
|        | Cdm    | New CDM with subset of tables. |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type               | Description                     |
|--------|--------------------|---------------------------------|
|        | TableNotFoundError | If any name is not in this CDM. |

### snapshot { #cdmconnector.Cdm.snapshot }

```python
Cdm.snapshot(compute_data_hash=False)
```

Snapshot CDM metadata. Executes and returns a 1-row pandas DataFrame.

Call with parentheses: ``cdm.snapshot()``. Without () you get the method
object, not the DataFrame.

Requires person, observation_period, cdm_source, and vocabulary tables.
Column order matches R CDMConnector snapshot(): cdm_name, cdm_source_name,
cdm_description, cdm_documentation_reference, cdm_version, cdm_holder,
cdm_release_date, vocabulary_version, person_count, observation_period_count,
earliest_observation_period_start_date, latest_observation_period_end_date,
snapshot_date, cdm_data_hash.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type   | Description                                             | Default   |
|-------------------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| compute_data_hash | bool   | If True, include data hash in snapshot (default False). | `False`   |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type             | Description                                          |
|--------|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pandas.DataFrame | Exactly one row (1-row DataFrame) with CDM metadata. |

### subset { #cdmconnector.Cdm.subset }

```python
Cdm.subset(person_id)
```

Subset the CDM to a set of person IDs.

Returns a new CDM where all clinical tables are filtered to rows
whose person_id (or subject_id) is in the given set. Requires
a database-backed CDM with write_schema.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                        | Description            | Default    |
|-----------|-----------------------------|------------------------|------------|
| person_id | list\[int\] or array - like | Person IDs to include. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                      |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------------|
|        | Cdm    | New CDM with tables subset to the given persons. |

### subset_cohort { #cdmconnector.Cdm.subset_cohort }

```python
Cdm.subset_cohort(cohort_table='cohort', cohort_id=None, verbose=False)
```

Subset the CDM to individuals in one or more cohorts.

Returns a new CDM where all clinical tables are filtered to persons
present in the given cohort table (optionally restricted by cohort_id).
Subset is lazy until tables are used.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name         | Type                       | Description                                                             | Default    |
|--------------|----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| cohort_table | str                        | Name of the cohort table in this CDM (default "cohort").                | `'cohort'` |
| cohort_id    | int or list\[int\] or None | Cohort definition ID(s) to include; None uses all cohorts in the table. | `None`     |
| verbose      | bool                       | If True, log subset size (default False).                               | `False`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                       |
|--------|--------|-----------------------------------|
|        | Cdm    | New CDM subset to cohort persons. |
